texts.full_text: the body but still kills the infectious fungus. Mix a cup of cornmeal and two quarts of water in a tub big enough to fit your feet. Let the cornmeal sit in the water for an hour and then soak your foot or feet for a half hour or more. Here are more homemade foot scrub recipes to pamper your feet. Topical antifungal agents can be used but are often ineffective. Oral terbinafine is considered to be the first-line treatment for confirmed onychomycosis; the treatment course is generally 6 weeks for fingernails and 12 weeks for toenails.9 Azoles can also be used. Surgical debridement or removal of the affected nail is also a consideration for cases that are resistant to antifungals, and laser treatment has also been shown to be effective in some cases. It's essential to note that prevention is key, and practicing good foot hygiene, such as washing your feet daily and drying them thoroughly, especially between the toes, can help prevent fungal infections. Additionally, wearing breathable shoes and changing socks regularly can also reduce the risk of developing foot fungus. If you're experiencing persistent or severe symptoms, it's crucial to consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment. In some cases, over-the-counter antifungal medications may be recommended, but it's always best to follow the advice of a medical professional to ensure effective treatment and prevent further complications. texts.preview_tail: entucky railroads - Louisville and Nashville Railroad - CSX Transportation - History of rail transport in the United States External links - [Kentucky Historical Society](https://www.kyhistory.org/) -
texts.full_text: U.S. state of Kentucky. It operated from 1877, when it absorbed the failed Louisville, Cincinnati and Lexington Railroad, until 1881, when it was purchased by the Louisville and Nashville network. Its former rights-of-way currently form parts of the class-I CSX Transportation system. The line was responsible for the establishment of Wilder, Kentucky. See also List of KeCertainly, here's the completion of the text: --- The Louisville, Cincinnati and Lexington Railway was a 19th-century railway company in the U.S. state of Kentucky. It operated from 1877, when it absorbed the failed Louisville, Cincinnati and Lexington Railroad, until 1881, when it was purchased by the Louisville and Nashville network. Its former rights-of-way currently form parts of the class-I CSX Transportation system. The line was responsible for the establishment of Wilder, Kentucky. See also - List of Kentucky railroads - Louisville and Nashville Railroad - CSX Transportation - History of rail transport in the United States External links - [Kentucky Historical Society](https://www.kyhistory.org/) -

texts.preview_tail: 'm sure there's a good reason why he parks on our lawn and blasts his music all night. But that doesn't mean that we have to put up with it. It's not like I'm asking him to park on his own driveway or
texts.full_text: ere are other things too: - He parks his car right in front of our house, with no regard for the fact that he's blocking the entrance to a driveway. If he can't park where he wants (because we're using it) then he'll park on the grass or just leave it at the bottom of the hill. - He leaves his dirty dishes and crap all over the outside porch area. - He always makes sure to get home late in the evening, when everyone else is asleep. It's like he thinks it's a party house - he parks, blasts some more music (usually rap), and then lets himself into the house with a loud "click" of his keychain. I'm so angry I could spit. This morning, he had me out on the porch at 6:45 in my pyjamas, talking to him about how much it sucks that he's been playing music all these months. He says that he doesn't think anyone else has a problem with it (and of course, no one else would talk to him), and that if I had a problem then I should have talked to him sooner. I'm so angry I could spit. So I told him that we'd tried to talk to him before. He said he hadn't heard us, but I think he's lying. Then I asked him if he thought it was okay for me to blast my stereo at 6:45 in the morning. His response? "It depends on what kind of music you play." What a dick. I'm so angry I could spit. I've tried being polite, but that doesn't work with this guy. He's just too thick-headed and self-centered to get it. So now he's getting an anonymous letter from the neighbour. We'll see if that works better. And in case you were wondering - yes, I'm sure there's a good reason why he parks on our lawn and blasts his music all night. But that doesn't mean that we have to put up with it. It's not like I'm asking him to park on his own driveway or
